en: Noise

Noise hazard identification must evaluate potential employee exposure. Workplace noise measurements must occur when exposure cannot be ruled out. Measurements must consider impulse noise.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 65(2)\nNoise and Vibration Regulation (VOLV), Section 6(2)
Exposure Assessment (Periodic, After Changes)

Pre-assignment hearing examinations must occur before tasks involving hazardous noise exposure. Periodic hearing examinations must occur for employees exposed to harmful noise levels. Examination frequency must reflect health risk.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 50(1)
Hearing Examination (Pre-Assignment, Periodic)
Exposure limits for noise must not be exceeded. Immediate corrective actions must apply when exceedances occur.\nNoise and Vibration Regulation (VOLV), Section 3(1)-(3)
Exposure Limits Not Exceeded (85 dBA)
Trigger values for noise must define when additional controls apply.\nNoise and Vibration Regulation (VOLV), Section 4(1), 9(3)
Actions for Limit Value Exceedance (80 dBA)

Noise reduction measures must lower exposure considering state of the art and source control.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 65(1,4), 65(4)5\nNoise and Vibration Regulation (VOLV), Section 11, 13(1)(1),(2)
Control Measures (Engineering, Administrative, PPE)
Hearing protection must be required in areas of hazardous noise exposure.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 65(4)3\nNoise and Vibration Regulation (VOLV), Section 14(1)
Hearing Protection

Noise exposure registers must list exposed employees and remain current. Exposure registers must be retained until exposure ends and submitted to the appropriate insurance authorities.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 65(4)6\nNoise and Vibration Regulation (VOLV), Section 14(5)
Exposure Register
High-exposure areas must be marked and, where justified, demarcated with restricted access.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 65(4)4\nNoise and Vibration Regulation (VOLV), Section 14(3)
Restricted Access Control

Noise hazard instruction must address risks and protective measures. Instruction must occur when trigger values are exceeded and must cover controls, limits, results, health effects, safe methods, and PPE use.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 65(4)(1)\nNoise and Vibration Regulation (VOLV), Section 8(1)
Training (Initial, After Exceedance)
